original_abstract: Imbalanced learning represents one of the most persistent and formidable challenges in data mining and machine learning. Despite decades of continuous advancement, the difficulty of learning from skewed class distributions remains a critical bottleneck, often limiting the practical utility of both traditional machine learning and modern deep learning models in real-world scenarios. Recent scholarship has made significant strides in this field, deepening our understanding of its fundamental nature while simultaneously uncovering new complexities. Given the rapid evolution of this landscape, this paper provides a comprehensive synthesis of recent breakthroughs and an in-depth review of existing strategies to mitigate class imbalance. Moving beyond traditional surveys that focus almost exclusively on classification, we expand our scope to include emerging techniques for regression tasks and the nuances of deep long-tail learning. Furthermore, we map the diverse practical impact of these methods across applications ranging from management science to engineering. Finally, we identify and discuss nascent challenges that define the next frontier of research in imbalanced learning.
humanized_abstract: Class imbalance is one of the longstanding and significant challenges in data mining and machine learning. Despite significant advances over several decades, the class imbalance problem still remains a major impediment to many applications of both machine learning and data mining techniques. State-of-the-art research in the area has been advancing the field by several folds in terms of understanding the class imbalance problem and its related challenges. This field is rapidly evolving, hence this article provides a summary of some of the recent research contributions and state-of-the-art methods proposed for tackling the problem of class imbalance, and acts as a survey of various techniques for balancing class distribution, specifically focusing on classification and also touching upon regression, as well as deep long-tail learning, while depicting the practical impact of these solutions in various fields such as management science and engineering, and providing an outlook of the emerging challenges in this area.
